channel.title: Xilinx on EasyFPGA
channel.description: Recent content in Xilinx on EasyFPGA

item.title: Custom RTL Design: TX & RX
item.description: Implementing UART TX and RX entirely in SystemVerilog: baud rate generator design, TX FSM, center-sampling RX FSM, and loopback testbench strategy.

item.title: UART Implementation Methods
item.description: Three practical ways to build UART on FPGA: vendor IP, soft processor plus UART IP, and custom RTL, with trade-offs in flexibility, performance, and development effort.

item.title: RS-232 and RS-485 for UART Systems
item.description: How UART logic maps to physical layers: RS-232 versus RS-485 wiring, voltage levels, distance, noise tolerance, and deployment trade-offs.

item.title: UART Frame Format
item.description: A practical guide to UART frame fields: start/data/parity/stop bits, LSB-first ordering, overhead, and throughput implications.

item.title: What is UART?
item.description: UART fundamentals for FPGA engineers: asynchronous communication basics, frame structure intuition, baud rate, clock drift, and practical use cases.

item.title: Understanding FPGA Speed Grades, Temperature Ranges, and Reliability Grades
item.description: What speed grade, temperature range, and reliability grade mean when selecting an AMD/Xilinx FPGA, and how to read the part number.

item.title: Implementing a DDR Memory Interface on FPGA with Xilinx MIG
item.description: Why external DDR memory is essential for FPGA designs, how to choose the right DDR type, and how to integrate it using the Xilinx Memory Interface Generator (MIG) IP.

item.title: Why AMD(Xilinx) Recommends Synchronous Resets
item.description:
https://docs.amd.com/r/2021.1-English/ug949-vivado-design-methodology/When-and-Where-to-Use-a-Reset

Advantages of Synchronous Resets

BenefitExplanationBetter Resource MappingSynchronous resets can be mapped directly to more FPGA resources (e.g., flip-flops inside DSPs, BRAMs).Improved Logic PerformanceAsynchronous resets can negatively affect general logic timing paths and increase routing complexity.Simpler RoutingA global asynchronous reset might not increase control sets but does require routing reset wires to many elements, which can become complex.Protects Memory StructuresAsynchronous resets may corrupt the contents of BRAMs, LUTRAMs, and SRLs during reset assertion—especially if those resources are driven by asynchronously reset registers.Placement FlexibilityWith synchronous resets, the logic can be more easily remapped during implementation for better packing and performance.Compatibility with Dedicated BlocksSome blocks like DSP48s and BRAMs only support synchronous resets, so using asynchronous resets can prevent proper inference into those blocks.

item.title: AXI with FPGA and SoC
item.description: AXI, or Advanced eXtensible Interface, is part of the AMBA protocol by ARM, designed for high-performance system designs in FPGAs and SoCs. It include

item.title: Trigger At StartUp
item.description: The Trigger at Start up feature is used to configure the trigger settings of an ILA core in a design programming file (.bit or .pdi) so that it is pre

item.title: Gigabit Ethernet with Xilinx FPGA and TEMAC IP
item.description: provides an overview of implementing Gigabit Ethernet using Xilinx FPGAs and the TEMAC IP core.

item.title: Vivado’s Text Editor
item.description: The Vivado IDE's Text Editor is less favored for coding compared to Vim and Visual Studio Code, which excel in shortcuts for tasks like find/replace a

item.title: Implementation of UART with Xilinx FPGA and AXI Uartlite IP
item.description: UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter) is a serial communication protocol that enables asynchronous data transmission between devices with

item.title: FPGA product lines
item.description: AMD's FPGA product families, categorized into UltraScale+, UltraScale, and 7 Series, utilize varying semiconductor processes (16nm, 20nm, and 28nm res

item.title: FPGA Manufacturers
item.description: The major FPGA manufacturers include AMD, Intel, Lattice Semiconductor, Microchip, and Efinix. AMD acquired Xilinx, Intel acquired Altera, and Microch
